summary: Iteration 35 — verified the public Work Order sign page fix (Darren's 'blank screen' bug). Backend public endpoints (GET /api/work-order/{token} and /api/work-order/{token}/pdf) return correct anonymous data for the real Dexter token. Frontend desktop (1280x800) renders Summary card with project name + total, dedicated 'Open Work Order PDF' button with target=_blank, embedded iframe visible (display:block). Frontend mobile (390x844) renders Summary card + Open PDF button at top, iframe parent is correctly hidden (display:none), and signature form fields are all reachable. All 8 backend pytest tests pass; all desktop + mobile UI assertions pass. The reported bug ('blank screen on mobile') is fully fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: WorkOrderSign.jsx: clean, well-commented (mobile iframe rationale documented inline). No issues. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL correctly, all interactive elements have data-testid, error states handled (toast.error on fetch failure, 'not found' fallback).

Suggestion (non-blocking): consider passing `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on the OPEN PDF link (currently only `noreferrer`). target=_blank without noopener is a minor security/perf concern but very low impact for a same-origin PDF link.
test_credentials: Public endpoint — no auth needed. Token 070FFFHvx78zluNbD3jN6l18KeX3igpI (Darren's real Dexter deal) used READ-ONLY. /sign POST was NOT called to preserve signature.
seed_data_creation: None — used real existing token in read-only mode.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_wo_public_sign_page.py can be reused as regression. Note: test_get_work_order_has_required_shape asserts already_signed=False — if the Dexter WO ever gets signed in production this test will need a fresh unsigned token. The TestUnknownTokenHandled test guards the 'work order not found' UX path. Frontend assertions exercised the responsive iframe-hide trick: desktop parent display=block, mobile parent display=none — confirmed via getComputedStyle.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE confirmed: iOS Safari (and most mobile browsers) refuse to render PDF inside an iframe — they show a blank rectangle. Darren opened the link on mobile, the 70vh iframe dominated the viewport with nothing in it, and he reported a 'blank screen'. The page was actually rendering correctly. FIX VERIFIED: (a) Summary card with project + total is now first in the DOM and visible above the fold on every viewport. (b) Prominent 'OPEN WORK ORDER PDF' anchor (data-testid='wo-sign-open-pdf') with target=_blank, href=`${REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api/work-order/{token}/pdf` opens the PDF in the OS native viewer — works on every device including iOS Safari. (c) iframe wrapper has Tailwind class `hidden md:block` → confirmed via getComputedStyle: desktop=block, mobile=none. (d) iframe height reduced 70vh → 60vh so the signature form is closer to the fold on desktop. Reproduction: open the URL on iPhone — user now sees header, summary, OPEN PDF button, and signature form immediately with no blank area. Mitigation deployed and verified.
